Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Compelling Plaintiffs Depositions, Responses To Discovery And Sanctions
Off Calendar Jan-24-2018 Denied
MINI MINUTES: No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT DAVID WISEBLOOD, ESQ.'s Motion For Order Compelling Plaintiffs Depositions, Responses To Discovery And Sanctions.

Defendant David Wiseblood's motion for an order compelling plaintiffs' depositions and responses to discovery and for monetary sanctions is denied. Plaintiffs Masha Levinson and Leon Taylor's request for sanctions is also denied. The primary asserted basis for the relief sought in the motion - that plaintiffs lied to the court and defense counsel about the scope of the destruction of their property as a result of the Sonoma County wildfires - lacks merit as previously adjudicated by this court on Mr. Wisebloods' sanctions motions. Mr. Wiseblood's decision to oppose an extension of the discovery cutoff dates when the trial date was continued shows that Mr. Wiseblood assumed the risk of not being able to take the depositions of the plaintiffs and thus it would be inequitable to allow him to do so by this motion, at least without providing plaintiffs with relief from the discovery cutoff dates for some or all of the discovery that they wish to take. While the motion is denied, it is not without substantial justification and thus no sanctions are awarded against Mr. Wiseblood.

Order signed. (see scanned order for details)

Judge: Harold E. Kahn, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, mtorreano@sftc.org, Reported. (D302/HK)

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Terminating Sanctions
Off Calendar Jan-04-2018 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Annalisa S. Zulueta, Esq. of G&P Schick PC, T:408.995.5050, for defendant and moving party;
Leon Taylor and Masha Levinson, T:415.519.8811, plaintiffs and opposing party appearing on their own behalf.

RULING: The matter was argued. At the conclusion of argument, the Court adopted the tentative ruling, as follows:

DEFENDANT DAVID WISEBLOOD, ESQ.'S Motion For Terminating Sanctions is DENIED. Mr. Wiseblood failed to show that there is any factual or equitable basis for the extraordinary relief he seeks. Even if there were some grounds to believe that plaintiffs Masha Levinson and Leon Taylor intentionally misled the court and/or Mr. Wiseblood about the damages the plaintiffs suffered as a result of the Sonoma County fires, that would still not be a sufficient basis to terminate this action since lesser sanctions would suffice to remedy the purported misconduct. However, the court is persuaded that Ms. Levinson and Mr. Taylor did not make any intentional misrepresentation. Mr. Wiseblood's accusations to the contrary and his efforts to obtain a litigation advantage not only are unfounded and violate norms of civility expected of all counsel, but are also arguably violative of CCP 128.5.

ORDER SIGNED. Judge: Harold Kahn; Clerk: G. Gonzales; Reporter: Giselle Casey CSR#8090, email: casey.giselle@gmail.com
